comment.text: The "RecoverTitle" translation key is used to display the text "Access recovery" in a modal dialog that allows users to recover access. This key appears in the header of a modal dialog and provides context for the user's interaction.

usage.context:
onClose={onRecoverModalClose}
 displayType={modalDisplayType}
 aria-labelledby="recover-access-modal-title"
 dataTestId="recover_access_modal"
 >
 <ModalDialog.Header>{t("Common:RecoverTitle")}</ModalDialog.Header>
 <ModalDialog.Body>
 <div className={styles.body}>
 <Text
 key="text-body"
 className={styles.textBody}

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Finnish translation 'Käytä palautusta' means 'Use recovery' (imperative verb), whereas the English 'Access recovery' is a noun phrase acting as a title for the recovery process/access.

The Lao translation 'ການເຂົ້າເຖິງການກູ້ຄືນ' literally means 'Accessing the recovery' or 'Access to recovery', which sounds like a process of accessing a recovery tool. In the context of a modal header for recovering access, it should mean 'Recover access'.
ai_spell_check_issues.suggestion: ການກູ້ຄືນການເຂົ້າເຖິງ

ai_spell_check_issues.description: The Dutch translation 'Toegang herstel' is a literal word-for-word translation that is grammatically incorrect in Dutch. It lacks the necessary connection between 'access' and 'recovery'.
The Russian translation 'Доступ к порталу' means 'Access to the portal', which is a different concept from 'Access recovery' (restoring lost access).

The Sinhala translation 'ප්‍රතිසාධනයට ප්‍රවේශය' translates to 'Access to recovery/healing' (often used in a medical or health context). In the context of account/access recovery, it should mean 'Recovering access' or 'Access recovery' (restoring the ability to enter the system).
The Serbian translation 'Приступи опоравку' means 'Proceed to recovery' or 'Approach recovery', which is grammatically awkward and semantically different from the noun phrase 'Access recovery'.

The Serbian translation 'Pristupi oporavku' means 'Accesses to recovery' (plural/verb form), whereas the English 'Access recovery' in a modal header context refers to the process of 'Recovery of access'.

The translation '访问恢复' means 'Access restoration' or 'Access recovery' in a sense of restoring a visit/accessing something, but in the context of account/access recovery (recovering one's ability to log in), '找回访问权限' or '恢复访问权限' is the standard technical term. '访问恢复' sounds like a literal, unnatural translation of 'Access recovery'.

The Albanian translation 'Qasja në rikuperimin' means 'Access to the recovery' (implying access to a recovery process/object), whereas the English 'Access recovery' in this context refers to the action of recovering access. The current translation is grammatically awkward and changes the semantic intent of the header.
